content: Token dual storage: localStorage(terminal_access_token) AND IndexedDB(settings via Zustand persist). onlineSync reads localStorage; txSync reads IndexedDB (settingsStore.deviceToken). Drift risk: refresh writes both, but activateTerminal writes localStorage only — first activation populates LS only, IDB deviceToken stays null until separate setDeviceToken call.

JWT has NO setExpirationTime in createTerminalJWT. Token never expires from JWT side. verifyTerminalJWT does jwtVerify without explicit exp check. tokenExpiresAt comes from server heartbeat response (DB-managed). Effective: stolen token is valid forever unless server revokes.

Offline payment flush reads deviceToken from settingsStore (IndexedDB). If token was never written there (only localStorage from activateTerminal), flushOfflineQueue returns 0 silently — offline queue NEVER flushes. Security audit goal but found a money-losing payment bug.
JWT tokens stored in plaintext: localStorage AND IndexedDB on disk (Electron userData/IndexedDB). No encryption at rest. Anyone with file system access reads bearer token.

Electron CORS bypass: main.js injects Access-Control-Allow-Origin:* for ALL responses missing the header. Plus Access-Control-Allow-Headers: Authorization. Any compromised renderer can call any cross-origin endpoint with the token.
displayWindow (secondary screen) loads from same origin via preload — no separate auth/session isolation. Receives data via display:send IPC. No auth handshake; trusts main window.

auto-updater downloads/installs without re-auth. quitAndInstall happens regardless of in-flight offline queue. If update fires before flushOfflineQueue completes, pending payments survive in sqlite + IDB (good) but heartbeat dropping mid-sync could leave records unmarked.

PIN: bcryptjs hash stored in settingsStore.config.adminPin (IDB). Default '1234' if not migrated. verifyPin compares bcrypt OR plaintext fallback. No rate-limit, no lockout. Brute-forceable from IDB or live UI.
No auto-lock / idle timeout for terminal. No session-invalidation on PIN re-entry failures. PIN protects admin config only; payment flow uses device JWT.

syncedIds contract: server returns explicit list, client only marks those. Sound. But if response is malformed (no syncedIds key) result.syncedIds??[] => 0 marked but failed=queue.length. Records remain pending and will be re-sent on next flush — duplicate-charge risk if server actually persisted them but failed to return ids.
